risks: Plan acknowledges that creating internal/language_tiers and adding GradeNA/Unsupported to domain types conflicts with project non-goals but does not resolve this contradiction — proceeding anyway introduces structural risk of rejected design
GradeNA value is ambiguous (255 or -1) with no resolution; if domain grades use uint8, -1 is invalid, and 255 may overflow grade arithmetic elsewhere
Plan does not audit for other callers of Score() beyond pipeline.go — changing its signature could break undiscovered call sites
TierForLanguage has no defined behavior for empty string, case sensitivity, or unknown languages beyond the explicit mapping — edge cases unaddressed
summary: Plan self-identifies architectural contradictions (non-goals forbid creating language_tiers/GradeNA yet proceeds) and leaves GradeNA value unresolved (255 vs -1), making it not technically sound as written.
